raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Double Charge for Cancelled XXXX XXXX Request for Assistance Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am writing to report a billing issue involving XXXX and my American Express account. On Saturday, XX/XX/year>2025, I contacted XXXX to request that they notify our hotel of a delay in our arrival. A representative advised me that, due to the change in travel dates, I would need to cancel my original reservation ( booked in XXXX ) and rebook the trip entirely. I agreed to this change after being assured that a credit of $ XXXXa portion of the original tripwould be issued to my American Express account. 

XXXX rebooked the entire trip and charged my account {$3400.00}. However, the promised {$2700.00} credit never appeared. As a result, I have been charged twice for a single trip that I ultimately did not take. 

I have initiated a dispute with American Express, but XXXX has not responded to my repeated attempts to resolve the matter. The only feedback I have received from them is that " no refunds are allowed on booked trips, '' which does not address the core issue : I was assured of a full credit prior to rebooking, and I never received it. 

While I may be able to recover a portion of the initial cost through travel insurance, I do not believe it is fair or lawful to be charged twice for the same tripespecially after receiving confirmation that a credit would be processed. 

I would appreciate any assistance your office can provide in investigating and resolving this matter. 

Thank you for your attention.
